data.content:
### Filesystem - Write File

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.3/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Writes data to a file in the sandbox filesystem. Can write a single file or a list of files.

```APIDOC
## POST /filesystem/write

### Description
Writes content to a file or a list of files in the sandbox filesystem.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/filesystem/write

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the file (for single file write).
- **data** (string|bytes|IO) - Required - Data to write to the file (for single file write).
- **files** (array of objects) - Required - List of files to write (for multiple files write).
  - **path** (string) - Required - Path to the file.
  - **data** (string|bytes|IO) - Required - Data to write to the file.
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user. Defaults to "user".
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example (single file)
{
  "path": "/path/to/your/file.txt",
  "data": "Hello, world!"
}

### Request Example (multiple files)
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file1.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 1"
    },
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file2.bin",
      "data": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    }
  ]
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **EntryInfo** (object) - Information about the written file or list of files.
  - **path** (string) - Path of the entry.
  - **type** (string) - Type of the entry ('file' or 'directory').

#### Response Example
{
  "path": "/path/to/your/file.txt",
  "type": "file"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem Write API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.3/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Write content to a file in the sandbox filesystem. Supports writing single files or multiple files.

```APIDOC
## POST /filesystem/write

### Description
Write content to a file or multiple files in the sandbox filesystem. Existing files will be overwritten. If the target path does not exist, an error will occur.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/filesystem/write

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path_or_files** (Union[str, List[WriteEntry]]) - Required - Path to the file or a list of files to write.
- **data_or_user** (Union[str, bytes, IO, Username]) - Optional - Data to write to the file (if path is provided) or user (if files are provided) (default: "user").
- **user_or_request_timeout** (Optional[Union[float, Username]]) - Optional - User (if data is provided) or request timeout.
- **request_timeout_or_none** (Optional[float]) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "path_or_files": "/path/to/file.txt",
  "data_or_user": "This is the file content."
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **result** (EntryInfo | List[EntryInfo]) - Information about the written file(s).

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "result": {
    "name": "file.txt",
    "path": "/path/to/file.txt",
    "is_dir": false
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem API - Write File

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.7.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Writes content to a file in the sandbox filesystem. Can create new files or overwrite existing ones. Supports writing multiple files at once.

```APIDOC
## Filesystem API - Write File

### Description
Writes content to a file on the path. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Directories are created automatically if they don't exist.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/filesystem/write

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **path_or_files** (string | List[WriteEntry]) - Required - Path to the file or a list of files to write.

#### Request Body (for single file write)
- **data** (string | bytes | IO) - Required - Data to write to the file.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user. Defaults to `user`.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

#### Request Body (for multiple files write)
- **files** (List[WriteEntry]) - Required - List of files to write.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user. Defaults to `user`.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **result** (EntryInfo | List[EntryInfo]) - Information about the written file or files.

#### Response Example (single file)
```json
{
  "result": {
    "path": "/path/to/file.txt",
    "type": "file",
    "size": 100
  }
}
```

#### Response Example (multiple files)
```json
{
  "result": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file1.txt",
      "type": "file",
      "size": 100
    },
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file2.txt",
      "type": "file",
      "size": 200
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem API - Write File

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Writes data to a file in the sandbox filesystem. Supports writing single files or multiple files.

```APIDOC
## POST /filesystem/write

### Description
Writes data to a file or multiple files in the sandbox filesystem. If the file or directories do not exist, they will be created (except for intermediate directories).

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/filesystem/write

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path_or_files** (Union[str, List[WriteEntry]]) - Required - Path to the file or a list of files to write.
- **data_or_user** (Union[str, bytes, IO, Username]) - Optional - Data to write to the file (if writing a single file) or the user to run the operation as. Defaults to "user".
- **user_or_request_timeout** (Optional[Union[float, Username]]) - Optional - User to run the operation as (if data is provided) or request timeout. Defaults to "user".
- **request_timeout_or_none** (Optional[float]) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example (Single File)
```json
{
  "path": "/path/to/your/file.txt",
  "data": "Hello, E2B!",
  "user": "user"
}
```

### Request Example (Multiple Files)
```json
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file1.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 1"
    },
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file2.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 2"
    }
  ],
  "user": "user"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Union[WriteInfo, List[WriteInfo]]** - Information about the written file(s).

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "writtenBytes": 15,
  "path": "/path/to/your/file.txt"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Write File Content with Filesystem (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Writes data to a file in the sandbox filesystem. Supports writing string, bytes, or IO objects. If the file does not exist, it will be created. If it exists, it will be overwritten. Also supports writing multiple files in a single operation. Returns information about the written file(s).

```python
class Filesystem()

@overload
async def write(path: str, data: Union[str, bytes, IO], user: Username = "user", request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> WriteInfo:
    # Write content to a file on the path.
    pass

@overload
async def write(files: List[WriteEntry], user: Optional[Username] = "user", request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> List[WriteInfo]:
    # Writes multiple files.
    pass

async def write(
    path_or_files: Union[str, List[WriteEntry]],
    data_or_user: Union[str, bytes, IO, Username] = "user",
    user_or_request_timeout: Optional[Union[float, Username]] = None,
    request_timeout_or_none: Optional[float] = None
) -> Union[WriteInfo, List[WriteInfo]]:
    # Writes content to a file on the path.
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem Write API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.1/filesystem/page.mdx

Writes content to a file in the sandbox filesystem. This operation can create a new file, overwrite an existing one, or create necessary directories if they don't exist.

```APIDOC
## POST /fs/write

### Description
Write content to a file. Writing to a file that doesn't exist creates the file. Writing to a file that already exists overwrites the file. Writing to a file at a path that doesn't exist creates the necessary directories.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/write

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the file.
- **data** (string | ArrayBuffer | Blob | ReadableStream) - Required - Data to write to the file.
- **opts** (FilesystemRequestOpts) - Optional - Connection options.

### Request Example (single file)
```json
{
  "path": "/path/to/new_file.txt",
  "data": "Hello, world!",
  "opts": {
    "requestTimeoutMs": 30000
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **WriteInfo** (object) - Information about the written file.

#### Response Example (single file)
```json
{
  "size": 13,
  "mtime": "2023-10-27T10:30:00Z"
}
```

---

## POST /fs/write (multiple files)

### Description
Write content to multiple files.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/write

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **files** (WriteEntry[]) - Required - An array of objects, each specifying the path and data for a file to write.
- **opts** (FilesystemRequestOpts) - Optional - Connection options.

### Request Example (multiple files)
```json
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file1.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 1"
    },
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file2.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 2"
    }
  ],
  "opts": {
    "user": "admin"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **WriteInfo[]** (array) - An array of objects, each containing information about the written file.

#### Response Example (multiple files)
```json
[
  {
    "size": 19,
    "mtime": "2023-10-27T10:35:00Z"
  },
  {
    "size": 19,
    "mtime": "2023-10-27T10:35:00Z"
  }
]
```
```
